location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday); up to 4 weeks remote per year

seniority.level: Director (people manager, senior IC-equivalent or above)
level_signals: 10+ years of experience in product management or management consulting
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers
Comp range $340K-$425K base, consistent with Director-level at a late-stage fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in product management or management consulting, ideally with direct responsibility for or experience scaling B2B products and GTM systems, with a strong understanding of growth, GTM, and/or marketing pipelines.
You demonstrate a strong aptitude for and actively use data to inform your decision-making. Basic SQL proficiency is strongly preferred.

You have proven experience working and communicating effectively with senior leaders and executives, and influencing stakeholders across departments and functions, including Engineering, Design, Marketing, Sales, CRMX/Business Systems, IT, Legal, and more. You excel at building trust and working relationships.

You are a systems thinker, capable of thinking holistically across product, technical, and people systems to identify bottlenecks and opportunities for efficiency, automation, fraud prevention, and product growth within the payables ecosystem.
Drive product and systems investments that improve GTM metrics such as CAC payback, pipeline conversion, and efficiency.
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers, fostering clarity, ownership, and a culture of high performance.
Define and execute a multi-quarter Growth strategy in partnership with Engineering, Design, GTM, Marketing, Ops, and Data to deliver high-impact, scalable products with measurable business impact.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Basic SQL proficiency is strongly preferred.
ideally with direct responsibility for or experience scaling B2B products and GTM systems

scope.surface: Growth Product — customer acquisition, onboarding, GTM tooling and automation, account scoring, TAM data, outbound automation
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech, enterprise and mid-market spend management
core_responsibilities: Drive product and systems investments that improve GTM metrics such as CAC payback, pipeline conversion, and efficiency
Define and execute a multi-quarter Growth strategy in partnership with Engineering, Design, GTM, Marketing, Ops, and Data
Embed within Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to identify workflow bottlenecks and lead automation and optimization initiatives
Analyze GTM funnel and conversion metrics to uncover insights and guide product priorities and roadmap decisions
Set clear goals, metrics, and success criteria for the Growth team
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers
Oversee core Growth Product and Engineering domains, including account scoring, TAM data, outbound automation, and GTM tooling
Align stakeholders around an aspirational 6–12 month roadmap
Ensure successful activation and implementation of customer initiatives by partnering with GTM and Customer Success

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($340K-$425K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Growth, GTM tooling, AI agents)
Concrete past examples of work provided (AI agents for GTM workflows, ML models for account prioritization)
Global company with tens of thousands of customers including well-known brands
Explicit 6-12 month roadmap alignment mentioned — signals strategic clarity
red_flags: Requires in-office 3 days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in SF, Seattle, or NYC — no full-remote option
Very high years-of-experience bar (10+) at Director level may narrow the pool significantly
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Role title includes 'AI' but responsibilities lean more heavily GTM/Growth automation; AI is secondary

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, people management responsibility, and 10+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer examples)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not exhaustively listed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on a number of factors including the candidate's location')
ambiguities: SQL proficiency is listed under Requirements section but qualified as 'strongly preferred' — treated as nice_to_have (soft) despite being in Requirements, since 'strongly preferred' signals preference not a hard gate. Also captured separately as a must_have note within the analytical_sense requirement.

Role title is 'Director of Product, Growth/AI' but JD body describes the role primarily as a Growth Product leader with AI as a tool/method rather than a dedicated AI product discipline — archetype classified as Growth PM with AI PM as secondary.
